policies.title: Data Retention & Deletion Policy
policies.description: Governs how long different data classifications must be retained and processes for secure deletion.
restrictions: Financial transaction data must be retained for 7 years minimum (tax compliance)
Personal data under GDPR must be deleted within 30 days of subject request
Operational logs retained 2 years, then archived to cold storage for 5 more years
AI training data derived from customer data requires explicit consent documentation

policies.title: GDPR & Privacy Compliance Framework
policies.description: Mandatory requirements for processing personal data of EU/EEA data subjects.
restrictions: All new features processing personal data require Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA)
Data transfers outside EEA require Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) in place
Consent must be freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous
Privacy by design is mandatory — personal data minimisation required at architecture stage
Automated decision-making with significant effects requires human review mechanism

policies.title: SOC 2 Type II Compliance Requirements
policies.description: Security controls required to maintain SOC 2 Type II certification for customer trust.
restrictions: All production systems must have audit logging enabled with tamper-proof storage
Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3) mandatory for all customer data
Multi-factor authentication required for all production system access
Vendor assessments required before integrating third-party tools with customer data access
Penetration testing required quarterly for customer-facing systems

policies.title: Open Source Software Policy
policies.description: Rules for using open source software in products and internal tools.
restrictions: Copyleft licenses (GPL, AGPL) prohibited in any code shipped to customers
All OSS dependencies must be registered in the approved software inventory
Security CVEs in dependencies must be patched within 14 days (critical) or 60 days (high)
AI model weights with non-commercial licenses cannot be deployed in production

policies.title: Change Management & Production Deployment Policy
policies.description: Controls for changes to production systems to prevent outages and maintain stability.
restrictions: All production changes require approval from at least one senior engineer and one product owner
Database schema changes require 48-hour review period and rollback plan
No production deployments during freeze windows: last week of each quarter
Major architectural changes (new services, databases) require Architecture Review Board sign-off
Automated rollback must be tested and documented for all changes affecting revenue-critical paths

policies.title: AI Ethics & Responsible Use Policy
policies.description: Governance framework for developing and deploying AI/ML features in products and internal tools.
restrictions: All AI features must undergo Ethics Review Board assessment before production deployment
AI-generated outputs affecting employment, credit, or legal status require human oversight
Training data must be audited for bias before model deployment
Customers must be clearly informed when they are interacting with AI systems
AI model decisions must be explainable on request — black-box-only deployments prohibited
AI features processing sensitive personal data require separate DPA with each customer

policies.title: Budget Approval & Procurement Policy
policies.description: Approval thresholds and procurement process for technology investments.
restrictions: Expenditure up to $50k: Department head approval
Expenditure $50k-$250k: Director + Finance approval required
Expenditure $250k-$1M: COO + Finance approval required
Expenditure above $1M: Board approval required
Multi-year SaaS contracts above $100k/year require legal review
All new vendor relationships require security questionnaire completion
